reasoning: The BAA10Y spread dropped sharply in mid-January 2026, reaching 1.63 on January 16, down from 1.75 earlier in the month. This strong downward momentum reflects aggressive early-year credit and risk asset buying, supported by reports of tight corporate bond spreads due to robust investor demand. While the spread is currently below its recent 1-month average (which sits near 1.70) setting up potential for short-term mean reversion (a bounce), the historical seasonality often sees spreads compress in January and February (in 2025, spreads hit 1.44-1.46 during these months). Weighing the sharp daily downward momentum against the mean-reverting properties of the spread, we slightly favor the spread remaining at or below 1.63 by Jan 25. For the further horizon (Feb 17), structural mean reversion toward the 1.68-1.72 range pulls the probability back closer to 0.5.
